title: hypericum undulatum subsp. boeticum
intro: Hypericum undulatum subsp. boeticum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species in the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Hypericum undulatum subsp. boeticum include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties also make it a popular ingredient in natural remedies for skin issues, such as eczema and acne.
